fab_section_md:
- Septon Eustace attended Tyland at his death and recorded the court's affairs, giving a version in which Aegon received the regents' decrees courteously (fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p01:31)
- He described the king at the Feast of Our Father Above as if made of wax (fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p01:111)
identity_line: Septon Eustace attended Tyland at his death and recorded the court's affairs, giving a version in which Aegon received the regents' decrees courteously.

fab_section_md: - Septon Bernard, a younger man of Unwin's blood, replaced Eustace but preferred composing sacred music to recording court gossip (fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p01:67)
identity_line: Septon Bernard, a younger man of Unwin's blood, replaced Eustace but preferred composing sacred music to recording court gossip.

fab_section_md: - The Sealord of Braavos boasted of replacing every ship Alyn had stolen or sunk, then, once placated, made peace and ceded the Stepstones though he did not hold them (fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p01:205)
identity_line: Sealord of Braavos — The Sealord of Braavos boasted of replacing every ship Alyn had stolen or sunk, then, once placated, made peace and ceded the Stepstones though he did not hold them.
